name: none
description: This effect is empty. It doesn't even have 'splash' or evolve...

name: change_diplomacy
description: This effect can be used to change the diplomacy stance of the source players with the target player.
It does NOT change the stance both ways.

attributes.name: diplomacy_state
attributes.description: The diplomacy stance to set.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose stance will be changed.

attributes.name: target_player
attributes.description: The target player whose diplomacy stance will be changed for the source player.

name: research_technology
description: This effect can be used to automatically research a technology for the specified player.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the technology will be researched.

attributes.name: technology_id
attributes.description: The technology which will be researched for the specified player.

attributes.name: force_technology
attributes.description: When enabled, force the selected technology to be researched, regardless if the civilization has access to it or not.

name: send_chat
description: This effect can be used to send a chat message to the specified player.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who will be receiving the message.

attributes.name: str_id
attributes.description: The string ID to use instead of the message.

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: The message to send to the player.

attributes.name: sound_name
attributes.description: The name of the sound to play when the message is sent.

name: play_sound
description: This effect can be used to play a sound at a specific location or globally for the specified player.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who will hear the sound effect.

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The tile to play the sound at. When not set, the sound will be played globally.

attributes.name: location_object_ref
attributes.description: The unit whose position and rotation will be used to play the sound at. When not set, the sound will be played globally.

attributes.name: global_sound
description: When enabled, the sound will be played regardless if the target is on screen.
This is useful for existing game sounds that are considered '3D'.
'3D' sounds are game sounds based on coordinates. '2D' sounds are sounds like GUI, button click or notification sounds.

attributes.name: sound_name
attributes.description: The name of the sound to play.

name: tribute
description: This effect can be used to send resources from one player to another.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The amount of the specified resource to send.

attributes.name: resource
attributes.description: The resource that will be sent.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who will be sending the resources.

attributes.name: target_player
attributes.description: The player who will receive the resources.

name: unlock_gate
description: This effect can be used to unlock specific gates.

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The gates to be unlocked.

name: lock_gate
description: This effect can be used to lock specific gates.

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The gates to be locked.

name: activate_trigger
description: This effect can be used to activate a specific trigger.

attributes.name: trigger
attributes.description: The trigger to be activated.

name: deactivate_trigger
description: This effect can be used to deactivate a specific trigger.

attributes.name: trigger
attributes.description: The trigger to be deactivated.

name: ai_script_goal
description: This effect can be used to communicate with an AI player by setting an AI Trigger number.
The AI Trigger number can be detected inside an AI script using: `event-detected trigger NUMBER`

attributes.name: ai_script_goal
attributes.description: The AI Trigger number to set

name: create_object
description: This effect can be used to create a unit (including buildings, heroes etc.) for the specified player

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The unit type to be created.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose unit will be created

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The tile where the unit will be created

attributes.name: item_id
attributes.description: The type of the unit that is selected in the dropdown

attributes.name: facet
description: The rotation of the created unit. This can be any integer between 0 and 15, with 0 looking towards the right of the screen
and 8 looking towards the left of the screen. With increasing values moving counter clockwise.

attributes.name: disable_sound
attributes.description: When enabled, disable the creation sound of the object

name: task_object
description: This effect can be used to order units to perform a specific action at a target location or on a target object.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to task

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will be tasked

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The tile to send the tasked units to

attributes.name: location_object_ref
attributes.description: The target unit (as if it was right clicked)

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will be tasked. When not set, units across the entire map are tasked

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: action_type
attributes.description: The type of action to perform on the affected units

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: disable_garrison_unload_sound
attributes.description: When enabled, disables the sound that plays when unloading garrisoned units

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

attributes.name: issue_group_command
attributes.description: When enabled, will issue a task as a group formation task, instead of every unit individually

attributes.name: queue_action
attributes.description: When enabled, the set task will be shift-queued

name: declare_victory
description: This effect can be used to declare victory or defeat for the specified player.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom victory or defeat will be declared

attributes.name: enabled
attributes.description: When enabled, victory is declared for the player. When disabled, defeat is declared

name: kill_object
description: This effect can be used to kill units.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to kill

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will be killed

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will be killed. When not set, units across the entire map are killed

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

name: remove_object
description: This effect can be used to instantly remove units from the map without triggering a death animation.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to remove

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will be removed

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will be removed. When not set, units across the entire map are removed

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_state
attributes.description: The state units need to be in to be affected by this effect

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

name: change_view
description: This effect can be used to move the camera view for the specified player to a specific tile.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The time in seconds it takes to scroll to the new camera position

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose camera view will be moved

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The tile to move the camera to

attributes.name: scroll
attributes.description: When enabled, animate the camera transition. When disabled, the camera snaps to the new position without any animation (ignoring the quantity)

name: unload
description: This effect can be used to unload units at a target location.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to unload

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose garrisoned units will be ordered to unload

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The tile where garrisoned units will be unloaded

attributes.name: location_object_ref
attributes.description: The target unit to unload at (as if it was right clicked)

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which garrisoned units will be ordered to unload. When not set, units across the entire map are ordered to unload

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

name: change_ownership
description: This effect can be used to change ownership of units from the source player to the target player.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to change ownership

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who currently owns the units

attributes.name: target_player
attributes.description: The player who will receive ownership of the units

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will have their ownership changed. When not set, units across the entire map have their ownership changed

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: flash_object
attributes.description: When enabled, flash the affected units when the source and target player are the same

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

name: patrol
description: This effect can be used to patrol units to a specific tile.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to patrol

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units to patrol

attributes.name: location
attributes.description: The tile to patrol to

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will be patrolled. When not set, units across the entire map are patrolled

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

name: display_instructions
description: This effect can be used to display a message in a panel for all players

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The unit whose icon to the display in the panel

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose color to use in the icon

attributes.name: str_id
attributes.description: The string ID to use as the instruction message instead of the message field

attributes.name: display_time
attributes.description: The number of real-life seconds to display the instructions panel for

attributes.name: instruction_panel_position
attributes.description: The position on the screen where the instruction panel will be displayed

attributes.name: play_sound
attributes.description: When enabled, plays the message notification

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: The instruction message to display in the panel

attributes.name: sound_name
attributes.description: The name of the sound to play when the instruction panel is shown

name: clear_instructions
description: This effect can be used to clear the message from an instruction panel.

attributes.name: instruction_panel_position
attributes.description: The position of the instruction panel to clear

name: freeze_object
description: This effect can be used to freeze units in place, preventing them from moving or taking any actions.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to freeze

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will be frozen

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will be frozen. When not set, units across the entire map are frozen

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

name: use_advanced_buttons
description: This effect used to toggle the advanced buttons in the minimap interface before the Definitive Edition. It is currently as useful as the None effect.

name: damage_object
description: This effect can be used to deal a specified amount of damage to units.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The amount of damage to deal to each affected unit

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to damage

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will be damaged

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will be damaged. When not set, units across the entire map are damaged

attributes.description: The building foundation to place

attributes.name: source_player

attributes.name: location

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will have their name changed

attributes.name: str_id
attributes.description: The string ID to use as the new name for the affected units

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will have their name changed. When not set, units across the entire map have their name changed

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: The new name to display for the affected units

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: max_units_affected

name: teleport_object
description: This effect can be used to teleport units to the specified tile.

name: display_timer
description: This effect can be used to display a countdown timer on the screen.

attributes.name: str_id
attributes.description: The string ID to use as the timer label instead of the message field

attributes.name: display_time
attributes.description: The initial value of the timer

attributes.name: time_unit
attributes.description: The unit of time to use (In game seconds, minutes or years (One year being 5 seconds))

attributes.name: timer_id
attributes.description: The ID of the timer to display (multiple timers can be shown simultaneously)

attributes.name: reset_timer
attributes.description: Removes the old timer if it exists, when disabled it will create multiple timers with the same ID, disable with caution

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: The message to display for the timer. Use '<TIMER>' to show the timer in your message

name: enable/disable_object
description: This effect can be used to enable or disable units.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The unit to enable or disable

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the unit will be enabled or disabled

attributes.name: enabled
attributes.description: When enabled, the unit is available. When disabled, the unit is not available

attributes.name: item_id
attributes.description: The type of the unit that is selected in the dropdown

name: enable/disable_technology
description: This effect can be used to enable or disable technologies.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the technology will be enabled or disabled

attributes.name: technology_id
attributes.description: The technology to enable or disable

attributes.name: item_id

name: change_object_cost

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the cost will be changed

attributes.description: The quantity of the third new resource cost for the chosen UNIT|TECHNOLOGY

name: set_player_visibility

attributes.name: target_player

attributes.name: visibility_state

description: This effect can be used to replace units with a different unit type.

attributes.description: The type of unit to replace

attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

name: modify_variable_by_variable
description: This effect can be used to modify a variable using the value of another variable.

attributes.name: variable
attributes.description: The variable that will be modified

attributes.name: operation
attributes.description: The operation to apply to the variable using the second variable's value.

attributes.name: variable2
attributes.description: The variable whose value will be used in the operation

name: counts_units_into_variable
description: This effect can be used to count units and store the result in a variable.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to count

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will be counted

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will be counted. When not set, units across the entire map are counted

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units with this class will be affected by this effect

attributes.name: variable2
attributes.description: The variable in which the unit count will be stored

name: add_train_location
description: This effect can be used to add an additional building where a specific unit type can be trained.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The unit type to add an additional training location for

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the additional training location will be added

attributes.name: object_unit_id2
attributes.description: The building type where the unit can now additionally be trained

attributes.name: button_location
attributes.description: The location of the button to use. This number is given by the following formula: (row - 1) * 5 + column

attributes.name: train_time
attributes.description: The train time used for the newly set train location

attributes.name: hotkey
attributes.description: The hotkey ID to use for setting the train location

name: research_local_technology
description: This effect can be used to research a technology locally for specific units, typically used for building-specific upgrades.

attributes.name: local_technology_id
attributes.description: The local technology to research for the specified units

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the local technology will be researched

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will research the local technology. When not set, units across the entire map research the local technology

attributes.name: object_unit_id2
attributes.description: The type of unit to research the local technology in

name: modify_attribute_for_class
description: This effect can be used to modify a specific object attribute for all units matching the specified class and type, globally for the specified player.

attributes.name: object_group2
attributes.description: The unit class to be affected by this effect

attributes.name: object_type2
attributes.description: The unit type to be affected by this effect

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will have their attribute modified

attributes.name: object_attributes
attributes.description: The object attribute to modify

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: The string value to use when the selected object attribute accepts a string (e.g. Object Name ID)

attributes.name: operation
attributes.description: The operation to apply to the attribute using the quantity.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The amount to modify the attribute by

attributes.name: quantity_float
attributes.description: The (decimal) amount to modify the attribute by

attributes.name: armour_attack_quantity
attributes.description: The amount to modify the ARMOR|ATTACK by

attributes.name: armour_attack_class
attributes.description: The ARMOR|ATTACK class to modify

name: modify_object_attribute
description: This effect can be used to modify a specific attribute on individual unit instances.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to modify the attribute of

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will have their attribute modified

attributes.name: object_attributes
attributes.description: The object attribute to modify

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will have their attribute modified. When not set, units across the entire map have their attribute modified

attributes.name: operation
attributes.description: The operation to apply to the attribute using the quantity.

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: The string value to use when the selected object attribute accepts a string (e.g. Object Name ID)

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The amount to modify the attribute by

attributes.name: quantity_float
attributes.description: The (decimal) amount to modify the attribute by

attributes.name: armour_attack_quantity
attributes.description: The amount to modify the ARMOR|ATTACK by

attributes.name: armour_attack_class
attributes.description: The ARMOR|ATTACK class to modify

name: modify_object_attribute_by_variable
description: This effect can be used to modify a specific attribute on individual unit instances using the value stored in a variable.

attributes.name: object_unit_id
attributes.description: The type of unit to modify the attribute of

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units will have their attribute modified

attributes.name: object_attributes
attributes.description: The object attribute to modify using the variable's value

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will have their attribute modified. When not set, units across the entire map have their attribute modified

attributes.name: operation
attributes.description: The operation to apply to the attribute using the quantity.

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: The string value to use when the selected object attribute accepts a string (e.g. Object Name ID)

attributes.name: variable
attributes.description: The variable whose value will be used to modify the attribute

attributes.name: armour_attack_class
attributes.description: The ARMOR|ATTACK class to modify

name: change_object_visibility
description: This effect can be used to change the visibility state of units for a specific player.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose units' visibility will be changed

attributes.name: target_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the unit visibility will change

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area in which units will have their visibility changed. When not set, units across the entire map have their visibility changed

attributes.name: visibility_state
attributes.description: The visibility state to use

attributes.name: max_units_affected
attributes.description: The maximum number of units affected by this effect

attributes.name: selected_objects
attributes.description: The units to be affected by this effect. When defined, overwrites all other unit filters, like area selection, unit type, object type etc.
